calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-26-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Maureen McFadden for Kathleen McEvoy (Administrator).

Judge Quinn disclosed that he believes he may have had dealings with Mr. Confrey at the Criminal Court, but it does not rise to any need to disqualify. Ms. McFadden addressed the request for reimbursement for the costs of Ellen going to the funeral stating that all of the Heirs have consented to pay for Sister Ellen's/Aunt Ellen's transportation and accommodations, made reference to Probate Code 10520(c) and stated that it could be treated as a preliminary distribution to all of the Beneficiaries. The Court questions if the Heirs knew it was coming from their distribution. Ms. McFadden stated that it's otherwise clear, that maybe they do not affirmatively know, that she does not think they would have any problem with it, that if the Court wants she can ask for consents or, it can come directly from Aunt Ellen's distribution, and that all of the beneficiaries agreed and wanted Aunt Ellen to be taken care of and that the expenses be paid. Ms. McFadden also stated that the Administrator had to hire people to help and that the Decedent was a hoarder. The Court stated that it doesn't take a Peace Officer to collect mail, that these are tasks to be done by the Administrator and that the estate should not be paying twice. Ms. McFadden stated that she has a revised order that reflects fractions instead of percentages, that those percentage amounts were rounded up, that they will most likely have to be rounded down at final distribution and that is should work itself out. The Court noted that there is one account with a balance of $1,400,000.00 and informed Counsel that it needs to broken up into multiple banks so that the funds can be insured. The First and Final Account, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
